subject: Re: FW: from Mort for Jeffrey
body:
what I ve asked for is a breakdown of the monies spent
as well as oweing..  including the assets still
remaining... my accounting department is working
diligently to figure out , how it is possible that so
many millions were spent compared to the  other mag
ventures,for so few issues,  ,,  the oversight of the
expenses, seemed not to exist,, and  I have been
extremely sensitive , to it geting out of control as ,
as you recall the continued funding if we had decided
to move forward , was to be carried by your group....
I asked the details of severance and told it was three
weeks.... how can that be 500,000 

--- Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com> wrote:

>  
> 
>   _____  
> 
> From: Clare Probert
> [mailto:cprobert@bostonproperties.com] 
> Sent: Wednesday, January 18, 2006 2:20 PM
> To: lesley@nysgllc.com
> Subject: from Mort for Jeffrey
> 
> 
> Dear Jeffrey:
>  
> Tom Peck tells me that you have not put forth the
> matching $500,000 which I
> have already put forward.  Last Friday we owed a lot
> of money to a lot of
> people. These are writers who deserve to be paid,
> they have 'kill' fees and
> things of this sort, amongst other things.  We have
> been trying to get this
> done and clean it up.  We are now held up by the
> non-payment and we're going
> to get a lot of bad press on it if we don't meet our
> obligations.
>  
> Please do respond A.S.A.P.  You've already seen the
> description of the
> liquidation values.  I've not only put in $500,000;
> I ate a considerable
> amount of money because the start-up costs for the
> circulation program far
> exceeded our budget and I felt the better part of
> valor would be to absorb
> it.  
>  
> I don't want to be in a position where we get our
> butts kicked for not
> meeting our obligations.   So please put up the
> money and let's get it over
> and done with.
>  
> Best,
>  
> Mort
>  
> _______________________________________

body_html:
what I ve asked for is a breakdown of the monies spent
as well as oweing.. including the assets still
remaining... my accounting department is working
diligently to figure out , how it is possible that so
many millions were spent compared to the other mag
ventures,for so few issues, ,, the oversight of the
expenses, seemed not to exist,, and I have been
extremely sensitive , to it geting out of control as ,
as you recall the continued funding if we had decided
to move forward , was to be carried by your group....
I asked the details of severance and told it was three
weeks.... how can that be 500,000

--- Lesley Groff lesley@nysgllc.com wrote:

From: Clare Probert
[mailto:cprobert@bostonproperties.com]
Sent: Wednesday, January 18, 2006 2:20 PM
To: lesley@nysgllc.com
Subject: from Mort for Jeffrey

Dear Jeffrey:

Tom Peck tells me that you have not put forth the
matching $500,000 which I
have already put forward. Last Friday we owed a lot
of money to a lot of
people. These are writers who deserve to be paid,
they have 'kill' fees and
things of this sort, amongst other things. We have
been trying to get this
done and clean it up. We are now held up by the
non-payment and we're going
to get a lot of bad press on it if we don't meet our
obligations.

Please do respond A.S.A.P. You've already seen the
description of the
liquidation values. I've not only put in $500,000;
I ate a considerable
amount of money because the start-up costs for the
circulation program far
exceeded our budget and I felt the better part of
valor would be to absorb
it.

I don't want to be in a position where we get our
butts kicked for not
meeting our obligations. So please put up the
money and let's get it over
and done with.

Best,

Mort

Clare Probert
Executive Assistant to Mortimer B. Zuckerman
